titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Wilfrid Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender, 17 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Wilfrid Spender

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This letter is part of a series of correspondence from Sir Wilfrid Bliss Spender to his wife Lady Lillian Spender. The letters date mainly from 1915 to 1918, when Sir Wilfrid was serving in France. The letters concern politics, the Irish question, personal life, and Wilfrid's experience of the Great War.
In this letter Spender is writing to his wife, Lady Lillian Spender, née Dean, (1880-1968). Sir Wilfrid Spender (1876 - 1960) was a member of the British Army until 1913 when controversy surrounding his signature of the Ulster Solemn League and Covenant resulted in his resignation. Spender was called up for military service in 1914 and from 1915 served in the 36th (Ulster) Division. Throughout the war, Spender remained in contact with the Ulster Unionist leadership, and consistently opposed Home Rule and the six-county solution.
In this letter, Spender very briefly discusses the business of his working day, the unfairness of recent moves towards Home Rule, and the weather. He also wonders how he will make it through the next three months without seeing Lillian.

msIdentifier.repository: Public Record Office of Northern Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Still
your
BW
WB
Spender 17 June Very sorry no letter was
possible today in time for post
& equally impossible now
but I am very well
tho' very busy. I was out with G'OC
to see my wolf hole &
it was quite touching to see
how nice it had been made I realized that it was not
quite useless having let the
RE know it was for me as
it has evidently been a little
labour of kind hearts & is
much better that the GOC!!
which is of course a long
way back. Farnham is back & had
hoped to give me definite news
today but it has not come. George was not authorised to
make the proposals for a settle-
ment now (!) it was not to
come in till the end of the war,
& then has been the dickens of
a row, & he may not now
get the other job. Nice weather again at last.
A jolly little note from Luddy
which I will answer later. I
hope. The Rummies are to begin
a big push today I believe. I am so sorry for such
a poor little scrap now but
you wouldn't blame me if
you were here I know. Wolf has not yet quite
forgotten a little pr, but I
expect he will do so if he doesn't
see her for another 3 months.
